what_it_does: Defines a React command palette overlay that opens when requested, builds a searchable list of canvases, actions, icon names, color tokens, and voice rules, filters them by text input, and executes navigation, export, copy, or close actions from mouse or keyboard interaction.
observations: Uses aliased React hooks from the global React object.
Builds the searchable item list only while the palette is open.
Combines static canvas/action/color/voice-rule entries with dynamic icon names from window.CV_ICONS.data.
Filters by substring match across label, kind, and hint, then applies simple label-based scoring.
Supports Escape, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, and Enter through a window keydown listener.
Focuses the search input shortly after opening with setTimeout.
Uses inline styles with CSS custom properties for layout, colors, typography, radius, and shadows.
standouts: The palette acts as a unified command surface across canvases, design tokens, icon names, and voice rules.
Search results carry executable action closures rather than separate command identifiers.
The UI includes inline visual affordances for icon glyphs, color swatches, item kind, keyboard hint, and result counts.
conventions: Uses global component registration instead of module export syntax.
Uses inline style objects rather than CSS classes.
Uses CSS variables for design-system values.
Uses optional chaining for access to window.CV_ICONS.
Uses array literals to define local command data.
Uses index keys when rendering filtered results.
concerns: The items memo dependency list only includes open, while the item actions close over nav, onClose, openExport, and copyText.
The keydown effect is re-created whenever filtered or sel changes.
ArrowDown can set sel to -1 when filtered is empty because filtered.length - 1 becomes -1.
The component depends on global React, CvIcon, window.CV_ICONS, and window.CommandPalette, which ties it to a browser/global runtime.
Using list index as the rendered key can make identity unstable as filtering changes.
notes: This file is the browser-global React implementation of a Cmd+K style search and action overlay for the ConceptV design-system workspace.
questions: Should edited colors beyond the static baseColors list be included, as suggested by the comment?
Is Cmd+K handled outside this file, since this component only handles keys while open?
Should the searchable data update while the palette remains open if icon data or callback props change?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a cross-canvas search overlay, though the file itself does not register the Cmd+K opener.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make disparate design-system resources quickly searchable and actionable from a single overlay without switching canvases manually.
novelty: It treats canvases, actions, icons, colors, and voice rules as one searchable command index with item-local rendering and actions.
contribution: This file contributes a compact command-palette interaction model that a fused system would keep as a unified search/action surface spanning navigation targets, export/build actions, icon-name copying, color copying, and voice-rule entry points.
summary_for_embedding: A browser-global React CommandPalette component that renders a searchable overlay for canvases, actions, icons, colors, and voice rules with keyboard and mouse execution.
intention_for_embedding: This component was for providing fast cross-canvas discovery and command execution inside the ConceptV design-system interface.

symbols.does: Renders and manages the command palette overlay, search index, filtering, keyboard state, and result actions.
symbols.description: CommandPalette receives open/close, navigation, export, and copy callbacks, then builds a local command index from hardcoded canvases, actions, base colors, voice rules, and global icon data. It renders a modal-like overlay with a focused search input, filtered result rows, visual glyph or swatch indicators, keyboard hints, and footer counts.

symbols.does: Handles Escape, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, and Enter key presses while the palette is open.
symbols.description: onKey is defined inside the open-state effect and attached to window as a keydown listener. It closes the palette on Escape, adjusts the selected result index with arrow keys, and invokes the active filtered item action on Enter.
